alerts.title: Iran may have used Chinese-supplied missile to down US fighter jet
alerts.summary: NBC News reports sources indicate Iran may have used a Chinese-made surface-to-air missile to shoot down a US fighter jet over Iranian airspace (~32.2°N, 52.8°E). If confirmed, the incident introduces direct Chinese hardware into the kinetic exchange and marks the first reported US combat aircraft loss of the conflict, with significant implications for great-power involvement.

alerts.title: Iran reopens 18 underground missile sites struck during US bombing campaign
alerts.summary: Times of Israel, CNN, Haaretz and Jerusalem Post report satellite imagery shows Iran has cleared and reopened most entrances to 18 underground missile facilities targeted in earlier US strikes (~31.8°N, 52.8°E). Iranian officials say the arsenal is again ready to fire across the Middle East, undercutting the strategic value of the US bombing plan and signaling residual second-strike capacity.

alerts.title: IDF captures Beaufort Castle in deepest Lebanon incursion since 2000
alerts.summary: BBC, CBC and Middle East Spectator (~33K views) confirm Israeli ground forces have seized Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon (~33.8°N, 35.58°E), the deepest IDF push into the country in over 25 years. ME Spectator reports the advance is being preceded by indiscriminate bombardment and alleged white phosphorus use, while Hezbollah FPV drones are inflicting growing IDF casualties.

alerts.title: Kuwait struck by missile; US declares Iranian ceasefire violation, launches fresh strikes
alerts.summary: PBS reports US Central Command has accused Iran of violating the ceasefire after a missile attack on Kuwait, prompting new US 'self-defense' strikes on Iranian missile sites and IRGC boats laying mines (~31.7°N, 53.2°E). LiveNOW from FOX adds that the US fired on a cargo vessel attempting to breach an Iranian port blockade, broadening the kinetic footprint beyond previously reported strike zones.

alerts.title: Kazakhstan offers to take custody of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile
alerts.summary: Financial Times, Kurdistan24 and Shafaq News, surfaced via global_metrics nuclear headlines, report IAEA Director General Grossi has revealed Kazakhstan is willing to hold Iran's 60% enriched uranium stockpile as part of a potential deal. The proposal emerges as Rybar (~121K views) reports Trump has hardened US terms in the revised draft sent to Tehran, with breakout time still estimated at 1–2 weeks.

analysis_note: Threat level holds at 9/10 as the US-Iran ceasefire visibly fractures—Kuwait struck, fresh US strikes launched, Iran's underground missile arsenal reconstituted, and a possible Chinese-missile downing of a US jet—even as Kazakhstan emerges as a potential nuclear off-ramp and Israel pushes its deepest into Lebanon since 2000.
